title: Kill Chain Autopsy
learning_objectives: Apply the Cyber Kill Chain framework to a real-world incident
Construct forensic attack timelines from incident data
Identify detection gaps across kill chain phases
scenario: A logistics company in Nairobi suffered a ransomware attack last month. The incident report has been declassified for training. Your job is to dissect the attack using the Cyber Kill Chain framework and produce a forensic timeline showing exactly how the adversary moved through each phase.

scenario_context.challenge: Reconstruct a ransomware attack using framework analysis
scenario_context.relevance: Ransomware attacks on African businesses are increasing rapidly. Understanding kill chain phases helps both attackers and defenders map real-world intrusions.

subtasks.title: Incident Report Review
subtasks.description: Read the provided incident summary. Identify and annotate all attacker actions.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Go through the report line by line. Highlight every action the attacker took. Note timestamps, tools mentioned, and targets affected. Don't skip any detail.
subtasks.evidence_required: Annotated incident report with highlighted attacker actions

subtasks.title: Kill Chain Phase Mapping
subtasks.description: Map every attacker action to a Kill Chain phase. Include timestamps where available.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Create a 7-column table: Reconnaissance, Weaponisation, Delivery, Exploitation, Installation, Command & Control, Actions on Objectives. Map each attacker action to the correct phase.
subtasks.evidence_required: Kill Chain mapping table with phase, action, timestamp, and evidence columns

subtasks.title: Detection Gap Analysis
subtasks.description: For each phase, identify whether the organisation detected the activity. Suggest missing detection capabilities.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: For each of the 7 phases: was it detected? If yes, how? If no, what detection capability was missing? Think about logs, alerts, network monitoring, endpoint detection.
subtasks.evidence_required: Detection gap analysis with recommended controls per phase

subtasks.title: Timeline Visualisation
subtasks.description: Create a visual timeline of the attack from initial access to impact, including attacker actions and defender responses.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use any visual format: flowchart, Gantt-style chart, or annotated timeline. Show the progression, dwell time between phases, and where defenders could have intervened.

reflection_prompt: The logistics company had no security team and relied entirely on an antivirus product. How would you prioritise building their detection capability from zero with a limited budget?

quiz.question: The Cyber Kill Chain has how many phases?
quiz.explanation: The Lockheed Martin Cyber Kill Chain has 7 phases: Reconnaissance, Weaponisation, Delivery, Exploitation, Installation, C2, Actions on Objectives.

quiz.question: Detection gap analysis is valuable because:
options: It identifies where defenders had no visibility into attacker activity
quiz.explanation: Detection gap analysis reveals blind spots in an organisation's ability to see attacker activity at each phase.

mentor_notes: Evaluate the student's ability to correctly map actions to phases. Common errors: confusing Delivery with Exploitation, or missing the C2 phase. The timeline visualisation quality is a good indicator of analytical maturity.
